description:
You are a capacity-planning engineer who has just received
    raw, per-container resource statistics exported from the
    orchestrator. Your goal is to transform those raw files into
    two concise, well-structured reports that management can read.

    Directory layout that already exists when you start:
      1. /home/user/raw_stats/
            ├── containerA.txt
            └── containerB.txt
      2. /home/user               – your homedir; you have write access here.

    The contents of each *raw* file are plain ASCII key/value pairs
    (one pair per line, exactly as shown below – do **not** alter the
    spelling of the keys):

        CPU: <float-percent>
        Memory_Used_MB: <integer>
        Memory_Limit_MB: <integer>
        Network_In_KB: <integer>
        Network_Out_KB: <integer>

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    TASK   (STEP-BY-STEP GOALS)
    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    1. Read every “*.txt” file located in /home/user/raw_stats/.
    2. For each container, calculate:
         • Memory utilisation (%)  = Memory_Used_MB / Memory_Limit_MB × 100,
           rounded to one decimal place.
    3. Compute cluster-wide (aggregate) totals for:
         • CPU(%)                – arithmetic sum of the per-container CPU %
         • MemUsed(MB)           – sum
         • MemLimit(MB)          – sum
         • MemUtil(%)            – (total_used / total_limit) × 100,
                                   rounded to one decimal place.
         • Network_In_KB         – sum
         • Network_Out_KB        – sum
    4. Create the directory /home/user/reports/ if it does not yet exist.
    5. Inside that directory, create the file capacity_summary.log
       with **exactly** the following ASCII format
       (every comma and space is significant for automated testing):

           CONTAINER CAPACITY SUMMARY REPORT
           Generated: 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z

           Container,CPU(%),MemUsed(MB),MemLimit(MB),MemUtil(%)
           containerA,17.5,256,1024,25.0
           containerB,42.3,512,2048,25.0

           AGGREGATE:,59.8,768,3072,25.0

       Notes:
       • Use the literal timestamp shown above
         (management wants reproducible reports).
       • After the header line “Generated: …” insert one
         blank line (single newline).
       • After the table data insert one blank line, then the
         “AGGREGATE:” line followed by a newline.
       • Numbers must preserve the decimal point exactly as shown.
    6. Still inside /home/user/reports/, create a second file named
       network_breakdown.csv with the exact contents:

           Container,Network_In_KB,Network_Out_KB
           containerA,10240,20480
           containerB,5120,10240
           TOTAL,15360,30720

       The totals must match the sums you already calculated.
    7. When you are done, confirm the two files have been created by
       listing /home/user/reports/ to standard output so the human
       reviewer sees:

           capacity_summary.log
           network_breakdown.csv

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    VERIFICATION CHECKLIST (what the grader will test)
    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    • /home/user/reports/ exists.
    • /home/user/reports/capacity_summary.log exists and its
      contents **exactly** match the block above, byte-for-byte.
    • /home/user/reports/network_breakdown.csv exists and its
      contents **exactly** match the block above, byte-for-byte.
    • The agent printed a directory listing that shows both filenames.
truth:
<!--
      Ground-truth data used by the autograder.
      DO NOT reveal any of this in the public task description!
    -->

    <!-- Raw source files that are already present when the task begins -->
    <initial_state>
        <file path="/home/user/raw_stats/containerA.txt"><![CDATA[
CPU: 17.5
Memory_Used_MB: 256
Memory_Limit_MB: 1024
Network_In_KB: 10240
Network_Out_KB: 20480
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/raw_stats/containerB.txt"><![CDATA[
CPU: 42.3
Memory_Used_MB: 512
Memory_Limit_MB: 2048
Network_In_KB: 5120
Network_Out_KB: 10240
]]></file>
    </initial_state>

    <!-- Files that must exist when the agent finishes -->
    <final_state>
        <file path="/home/user/reports/capacity_summary.log"><![CDATA[
CONTAINER CAPACITY SUMMARY REPORT
Generated: 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z

Container,CPU(%),MemUsed(MB),MemLimit(MB),MemUtil(%)
containerA,17.5,256,1024,25.0
containerB,42.3,512,2048,25.0

AGGREGATE:,59.8,768,3072,25.0
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/reports/network_breakdown.csv"><![CDATA[
Container,Network_In_KB,Network_Out_KB
containerA,10240,20480
containerB,5120,10240
TOTAL,15360,30720
]]></file>
    </final_state>
